SEARCH
Package org.key_project.util.java

Class StringUtil

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.util.java.StringUtil
public final class StringUtil extends Object
Provides static methods to work with strings.

    • EMPTY_STRING

      public static final String EMPTY_STRING
      Constant for an empty string.
      See Also:

    • NEW_LINE

      public static final String NEW_LINE

      public static final String LATIN_ALPHABET_BIG
      The latin alphabet with big capitals.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • LATIN_ALPHABET_SMALL

      public static final String LATIN_ALPHABET_SMALL
      The latin alphabet with small capitals.

    • ADDITIONAL_ALLOWED_FILE_NAME_SYSTEM_CHARACTERS

      public static final char[] ADDITIONAL_ALLOWED_FILE_NAME_SYSTEM_CHARACTERS
      Additional characters allowed in file systems.

      It is important that ':' is not contained because "::" is replaced with a '/' by the JFileChooser under Mac OS.

    • NUMERALS

      public static final String NUMERALS
      The numerals.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • WHITESPACE

      public static final String WHITESPACE
      All characters representing whitespace.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • isEmpty

      public static boolean isEmpty(@Nullable String text)
      Checks if the String is empty.
      Parameters:
      text - The text to check.
      Returns:
      true = text is null or empty, false = text is not empty.

    • isTrimmedEmpty

      public static boolean isTrimmedEmpty(@Nullable String text)
      Checks if the trimmed String is empty.
      Parameters:
      text - The text to check.
      Returns:
      true = text is null or trimmed empty, false = text is not empty.

    • trim

      public static @Nullable String trim(@Nullable String text)
      Nullpointer save execution of String.trim()
      Parameters:
      text - The text.
      Returns:
      The trimmed text.

    • toLowerCase

      public static @PolyNull String toLowerCase(@PolyNull String text)
      Nullpointer save execution of String.toLowerCase().
      Parameters:
      text - The text to convert.
      Returns:
      The text in lower case or null if the given text is null.

    • createIgnoreCaseComparator

      public static Comparator<@Nullable String> createIgnoreCaseComparator()
      Creates a Comparator that can be used to compute the equality of two given Strings ignoring the case via String.compareToIgnoreCase(String). If both values are null also 0 is returned in Comparator.compare(Object, Object). If only one value is null Comparator.compare(Object, Object) will return a value different to 0.
      Returns:
      The created Comparator.

    • repeat

      public static String repeat(String text, int repetitions)
      Creates a line which consists of the given text.
      Parameters:
      text - The text to repeat.
      repetitions - The number of repetitions.
      Returns:
      The created line.

    • contains

      public static boolean contains(@Nullable String string, @Nullable CharSequence substring)

      Checks if the given string contains the substring.

      Attention: The empty string is contained in every string.

      Parameters:
      string - The string that should contain the substring.
      substring - The substring to check.
      Returns:
      true strings are not null and the string contains the substring, false if at least one string is null or the string does not contain the substring.

    • wrapLines

      public static @NonNull String wrapLines(@NonNull String string, int length)
      Wrap lines in a string. It replaces ' ' by '\n' such that (in general) every line is at most length characters long. If there are no spaces within length characters, then the long strings will not be broken and lines may be longer.
      Parameters:
      string - the string to break. May contain spaces and newline characters
      length - a positive number
      Returns:
      a string of the same length as the input in which some ' ' have been replaced by '\n'

    • wrapLines

      public static @NonNull String wrapLines(@NonNull String string)
      Wrap lines in a string. It replaces ' ' by '\n' such that (in general) every line is at most 100 characters long. If there are no spaces within 100 characters, then the long strings will not be broken and lines may be longer.
      Parameters:
      string - the string to break. May contain spaces and newline characters
      Returns:
      a string of the same length as the input in which some ' ' have been replaced by '\n'

    • toSingleLinedString

      public static @Nullable String toSingleLinedString(@Nullable String text)
      Converts the optional multi lined String in a single lined String by replacing all line breaks and tabs with a space.
      Parameters:
      text - The text to convert.
      Returns:
      The single lined text.

    • replaceAll

      public static @Nullable String replaceAll(@Nullable String text, char[] toSearch, char toReplace)
      Replaces all occurrences of a search sign with the replacement sign.
      Parameters:
      text - The text to search and replace in.
      toSearch - The signs to search.
      toReplace - The sign to replace with.
      Returns:
      The new created String.

    • equalIgnoreWhiteSpace

      public static boolean equalIgnoreWhiteSpace(@Nullable String first, @Nullable String second)
      Checks the equality of the given Strings ignoring whitespace.
      Parameters:
      first - The first String.
      second - The second String.
      Returns:
      true equal ignoring whitespace, false different.

    • fillString

      public static @NonNull String fillString(@Nullable String text, char leadingCharacter, int length) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Fills the given text with the leading character until it has the defined length.
      Parameters:
      text - The text to fill.
      leadingCharacter - The leading character to use.
      length - The length to fill up to.
      Returns:
      The created text.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If the text is already longer as the given length

    • trimRight

      public static @PolyNull String trimRight(@PolyNull String text)
      Performs a trim only on the right side.
      Parameters:
      text - The text to trim its right side.
      Returns:
      The trimmed text.

    • chop

      public static @PolyNull String chop(@PolyNull String text, int maxLength)
      Chops the given text if required.
      Parameters:
      text - The text to check.
      maxLength - The maximal length to ensure.
      Returns:
      The text considering the maximal length.

    • startsWith

      public static boolean startsWith(@Nullable Object obj, @Nullable String prefix)
      Checks if the given Object is a String which starts with the given prefix.
      Parameters:
      obj - The Object to check.
      prefix - The prefix to check for.
      Returns:
      true Object is String with given prefix, false otherwise.

    • isNumber

      public static boolean isNumber(@NonNull String val)

    • trim

      public static @NonNull String trim(@NonNull String text, @NonNull Predicate<Character> predicate)
      Returns a string that first and last characters violates the given predicate. Works similar to String.trim() but allows to specify the characters that should be consider for removal. The given predicate test the characters, if true the character is removed.

    • trim

      public static @NonNull String trim(@NonNull String text, char c)
      Removes the given character c from the prefix/suffix of the given string.
      See Also:
      • trim(String, Predicate)

    • trim

      public static @NonNull String trim(@NonNull String text, @NonNull String chars)
      Removes the given characters (in chars) from the prefix/suffix of the given string.
      See Also:
      • trim(String, Predicate)

    • replaceNewlines

      public static @NonNull String replaceNewlines(@NonNull String text, String with)
      Replaces newlines.
      Parameters:
      text - the text
      with - with
      Returns:
      the normalized text.

    • containsWholeWord

      public static boolean containsWholeWord(@NonNull String s, @NonNull String word)
      Checks whether a string contains another one as a whole word (i.e., separated by whitespaces or a semicolon at the end).
      Parameters:
      s - string to search in
      word - string to be searched for

    • isJMLComment

      public static boolean isJMLComment(@NonNull String comment)
      There are different kinds of JML markers. See Section 4.4 "Annotation markers" of the JML reference manual.
      Parameters:
      comment -
      Returns:

    • count

      public static int count(String text, int beginIndex, int endIndex, char x)
      Count occurences of character x in text, starting at beginIndex and ending at endIndex (exclusive).
      Parameters:
      text - text
      beginIndex - start index (inclusive)
      endIndex - end index (exclusive)
      x - character to search for
      Returns:
      number of times x is present

    • move

      public static String move(@NonNull String text, int line, int charPositionInLine)
      Parameters:
      text -
      line -
      charPositionInLine -
      Returns:

    • takeUntil

      public static String takeUntil(String content, String regex)

    • takeAfter

      public static String takeAfter(String content, String regex)

    • ascii2html

      public static StringBuilder ascii2html(String sb)
      Replaces <,>,& and new lines with their HTML masks.
      Parameters:
      sb - The StringBuffer with forbidden HTML characters
      Returns:
      A new StringBuffer with the masked characters.

    • removeEmptyLines

      public static String removeEmptyLines(String string)

    • appendRepeated

      public static void appendRepeated(StringBuilder b, char value, int count)
      Efficient way to append repeated characters to a StringBuilder instead of String::repeat. Allocates only once instead of twice and does half the work
      Parameters:
      b - the builder to append to
      value - the character
      count - the times to repeat the character
